title: Paths, File Admission, Root Boundaries, and Size Budgets · Program Defense
summary: A release reviewer compares near-miss decisions for paths, file admission, root boundaries, and size budgets and accepts only runnable changed-case evidence, explicit limitations, and named browser, filesystem, terminal, packaging, operating-system, load, security, and release transfer gates. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for bounded file-admission policy. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and eviden…
objectives: Accept PathLike input, preserve the user-facing path, and distinguish lexical path composition from filesystem resolution.
Verify existence, regular-file type, readability, and race-aware open behavior before analysis.
Enforce an explicit allowed-root and symlink policy using resolved ancestry evidence rather than prefix strings.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-bookbot-python cumulative Unicode-safe text-analysis portfolio.

Browser execution uses original fixed in-memory fixtures and deterministic pure-Python models only.
Host files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, package installation, native profiling, load,
operating-system races, publication, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class TextPolicy:
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    normalization: str = "NFC"
    casefold: bool = True
    top_k: int = 10


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    corpus_id: str
    revision: str
    policy_version: str
    command_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Bookbot portfolio ready"

For diagnose the hidden failure, run one convenient ASCII text, omit corpus authority, repository and policy identity, bytes, encoding, Unicode, offsets, reconciliation, ties, stderr, status, changed cases, tests, recovery, and native transfer, then accept a plausible report as Bookbot proof. This does not satisfy File Identity for bounded file-admission policy.
Run the File Identity boundary case for bounded file-admission policy, inspect the actual mechanism, then accept or repair the claim.

steps.title: Bookbot and Python Defend one difficult decision · Quiz File Identity
steps.instruction:
Write the quiz evidence note for File Identity: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A quiz Bookbot team handles case 8816 during a large-file migration rehearsal. Build deterministic Python evidence to admit a regular file inside an allowed root under an explicit byte budget; record the first failed boundary, causal evidence, repair, regression test, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner; then install the built wheel in a fresh environment and rerun success, invalid-argument, and decode-failure cases. Browser code uses pure bounded functions and original fixed fixtures only. Real files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, packages, installations, native profiling, operating-system races, load, publication, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Record stable path, size, modification observation, digest purpose, and open-file identity limits for reproducible analysis.

Evidence target: Bookbot and Python Defend one difficult decision · Quiz File Identity.
steps.why: Retrieving File Identity through the concrete bounded file-admission policy case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For Bookbot and Python Defend one difficult decision · Quiz File Identity, keep File Identity connected to executable Python text-analysis, command, test, package, and release decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Record stable path, size, modification observation, digest purpose, and open-file identity limits for reproducible analysis.

hints: For Bookbot and Python Defend one difficult decision · Quiz File Identity, name the stakeholder, corpus authority, revision, path, bytes, encoding, Unicode, token, count, rank, report, command, test, package, release, and transfer boundary, File Identity precondition, or invariant that must hold.

Build deterministic Python evidence to admit a regular file inside an allowed root under an explicit byte budget; record the first failed boundary, causal evidence, repair, regression test, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner; then install the built wheel in a fresh environment and rerun success, invalid-argument, and decode-failure cases. Browser code uses pure bounded functions and original fixed fixtures only. Real files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, packages, installations, native profiling, operating-system races, load, publication, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Record stable path, size, modification observation, digest purpose, and open-file identity limits for reproducible analysis.

For Bookbot and Python Defend one difficult decision · Quiz File Identity, challenge the documented misconception with changed-file, changed-encoding, Unicode, token, ranking, stream, CLI, test, package, security, accessibility, failure, recovery, and controlled native-transfer evidence: A filename uniquely identifies unchanged content over time.
